data.description: Phase 0: Study all Asimov Collective project sites, reverse-engineer their tech stack (frameworks, fonts, animation libraries, hosting), document the Asimov signature (typography, color, spacing, grid, animation, content structure, imagery, tone). Julianna identifies specific sites/pages/elements she loves. Deliverable: research/01-asimov-collective-analysis.md
data.title: Asimov Collective deep research — reverse-engineer stack, design signatures, catalog sites

data.description: Phase 1a: Deep study of Porter Robinson sites and Active Theory work. Catalog interactive elements, reverse-engineer tech (Three.js, WebGL, GSAP). Define the play-within-polish spectrum. Research already started — see research/02-porter-robinson-interactive-research.md. Deliverable: finalized research doc with recommendations for interactivity level.
data.title: Porter Robinson & interactive web research

data.description: Phase 1b: Gather graphics/illustrations/textures Julianna loves. Compile moodboard. Research AI graphics platforms (Midjourney, Codex, Gemini, DALL-E) for generating assets in the Asimov aesthetic. Test each platform, establish repeatable workflow. Deliverable: research/03-ai-graphics-pipeline.md + initial generated assets.
data.title: Visual inspiration gathering & AI graphics pipeline

data.description: Phase 2: Develop studio/practice name capturing the architecture + software + AI intersection. Domain availability check. Define visual identity basics (typefaces, color palette, logo/wordmark). Write one-line positioning statement. Register domain. Deliverable: brand guide.
data.title: Brand identity & naming

data.description: Choose tech stack based on Asimov research (likely Next.js or Astro). Set up project scaffolding, design system foundations (typography scale, spacing, color tokens). Configure deployment (Vercel or Netlify). Deliverable: working project skeleton deployed to staging.
data.title: Tech stack selection & project setup

data.description: Phase 3: Build all pages — Home (with Now page preview + portfolio highlights), About, Work: Architecture, Work: Software & AI, Writing, Now (studio desk). Implement smooth scroll, interaction patterns from Asimov research. Architecture portfolio with intentional sequencing and scale. Deliverable: all pages built and functional.
data.title: Design & build site pages

data.description: Phase 4: Pull bio, photo, writing from existing site. Curate and sequence architecture portfolio projects. Document software/AI projects. Write new copy (homepage statement, project descriptions). Generate initial AI graphics. Deliverable: site populated with all content.
data.title: Content migration & population

data.description: Phase 5: Build the /update-site Claude skill. Quick mode (one-liner input) and developed mode (rich input with images/links). Content generation pipeline: user input to formatted MDX. Deployment pipeline: new content to git commit to auto-deploy. Test full loop: skill to live site in under 5 minutes. Deliverable: working Claude skill + docs.
data.title: Claude skill for daily updates

data.description: Phase 6: Side-by-side comparison with Asimov Collective sites. Typography refinement, animation polish, mobile responsiveness, performance optimization, cross-browser testing. Deliverable: site at ship quality.
data.title: Refinement & polish to Asimov quality

Needs: visuals showing before/after riso treatment, description of the pipeline, process blog on building it. Meta: the tool that makes the site's visuals is itself a portfolio piece.

Address instructions from team-review-critical
Follow team review standard instructions

data.description:
The hero section doesn't fully deliver Asimov Collective-level visual impact. Two improvements needed:

1. **Bolder plane merge animation:** The two folding planes (architecture photos left, wireframe right) need to feel more dramatic and apparent as they merge. Currently the fold/merge feels subtle — it should be rich, bold, and unmistakable. Consider: stronger perspective distortion, more aggressive rotation range, visual cues that emphasize convergence (shadow, depth, overlap effects), timing/easing refinements.

2. **Perspective grid to define 3D space:** Add a receding grid (or similar spatial reference) that shares the same vanishing point / perspective as the folding planes. This grounds the planes in a defined 3D environment rather than floating on a flat cream background. The grid should reinforce the fold direction and make the depth feel intentional and architectural.

**Current implementation:** Canvas 2D with quadrilateral mapping (no WebGL). Two planes fold inward on scroll (0→1→0 rotation over 200vh). Left plane = photo slideshow, right plane = procedural wireframe. Parallax on mouse move. See `src/lib/hero-canvas.ts` (~490 lines) and `src/components/interactive/HeroBrandVisual.tsx`.

**Quality bar:** Asimov Collective — bold, confident, editorial. The hero should feel like a statement piece, not a tech demo.

**Reference:** research/01-asimov-collective-analysis.md for Asimov visual language.
data.title: Improve hero animation — bolder plane merge + perspective grid

data.description:
There is a bug in the scroll-driven animation on the hero section (HeroBrandVisual). The scroll animation effect is broken — this is critical and should be fixed before any visual improvements (ASMV-39).

**Key files:**
- `src/components/interactive/HeroBrandVisual.tsx` (scroll handler, lines 54-88)
- `src/lib/hero-canvas.ts` (rotation/plane geometry)

**Priority:** Critical — the hero is the first thing visitors see. A broken scroll animation undermines the entire landing experience.

**Note:** Bug details TBD — needs investigation to identify exact symptoms and root cause.
data.title: Fix scroll animation bug on hero

data.description:
Two improvements to the Now / Studio Desk page:

1. **Visual background:** The studio desk section needs a visual background treatment — something that gives it atmosphere and spatial presence rather than sitting on a plain surface.

2. **Refine artifact hover effect:** The hover animation on artifacts is good and should be kept, but the content bar/overlay that pops up on hover should be removed. Keep the motion/animation, lose the info bar.

**Key files:** `src/app/now/page.tsx` and any artifact/card components in `src/components/`.
data.title: Update studio desk: visual background + refine artifact hover

data.description: ArtifactBar.tsx:168 uses text-vermillion hover:text-vermillion-light but both resolve to #f65058 in tailwind.config.ts:21. The hover is a no-op — zero visual change. Fix: use text-scarlet hover:text-scarlet-dark for actual hover feedback.
data.title: Fix ArtifactBar hover state (vermillion-light = vermillion, no visual feedback)

data.description: Header.tsx nav opens on hover only (handleMouseEnter). Touch devices cannot hover. Cross button is tappable (40x40) but at 25% opacity with no label — not discoverable. Mobile users past the hero section have no way to navigate except scrolling to the footer. Need a mobile-friendly nav pattern (hamburger, bottom nav, or improved cross affordance).
data.title: Add mobile navigation pattern (nav unreachable on touch after hero)

data.description: brand-guide.md §3 (lines ~101-198) and §7 (lines ~389-481) describe the OLD pre-Riso palette (Terracotta, Amber, Cream, Coral, Vermillion). The actual palette is Riso: Paper (#FDFCEA), Oxblood (#471f20), Scarlet (#f65058), Olive (#b49f29), Moss (#68724d), Spruce (#4a635d). §5 favicon also references old Terracotta. Rewrite to match tokens.css and tailwind.config.ts. Found by all 4 review agents (B2, C5).
data.title: Rewrite brand-guide.md §3 (Color Palette) and §7 (Design Tokens) for Riso palette

data.description: hero-canvas.ts:68-69 hardcodes BG_COLOR=#F5F0E8 (old Cream, should be #FDFCEA Paper) and INK_RGB=44,40,36 (old Ink, should be 71,31,32 Oxblood). HeroBrandVisual.tsx lines 189,205,219,233,241 hardcode rgba(44,40,36,...) — should be rgba(71,31,32,...). Both render with pre-Riso colors. Found by all 4 agents (B3, I1).
data.title: Update hardcoded old palette colors in hero-canvas.ts and HeroBrandVisual.tsx

data.description: Legacy alias class names (vermillion, bg-cream, bg-forest) used across many files. Also globals.css ::selection uses --color-vermillion/--color-cream. All resolve correctly via aliases but should use canonical names. Search-replace: vermillion→scarlet, bg-cream→bg-paper, bg-forest→bg-spruce, --color-vermillion→--color-scarlet, --color-cream→--color-paper. Files: page.tsx, work/*.tsx, writing/page.tsx, ArtifactBar.tsx, StudioDeskScroll.tsx, globals.css. Found by all 4 agents (C2, C3).
data.title: Replace legacy color aliases with canonical Riso names across codebase

data.description: AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md have ~60-70% overlap and have already diverged into contradictions (typography roles were backwards in AGENTS.md). Options: (a) make AGENTS.md a thin wrapper that defers to CLAUDE.md, (b) eliminate AGENTS.md entirely. Current two-source approach guarantees future drift. Found by all 4 review agents (I5). Needs human decision on approach.
data.title: Resolve AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md duplication — single source of truth

data.description: Hero canvas renders 80 slices × 2 during crossfade = 160 drawImage calls/frame plus wireframe rendering. No quality degradation path for mobile devices. User reports noticeable jank on mobile. Investigate: reduce slice count on mobile, skip crossfade, use requestAnimationFrame throttling, or degrade to static image below a viewport threshold. File: src/lib/hero-canvas.ts. Found by Claude Critical review (C9), confirmed by user.
data.title: Optimize hero canvas performance on mobile

data.description: Footer.tsx:17-21 duplicates the same SVG grain texture that exists in globals.css:103-110 (.grain-overlay::before). Should use the existing .grain-overlay class pattern instead of inline SVG. Found by Claude Standard review (C6).
data.title: Extract Footer grain overlay to shared utility

data.description: About page was intentionally removed from header nav during axis nav redesign (4 balanced links: Architecture, Now, Writing, Software). But docs/plans may still reference it as a required page. Check PRD, CLAUDE.md, brand-guide.md, Lattice tasks, and any wireframe/plan files. Remove or update references that imply About needs to be in the nav. The /about route still exists — it's just not in the header.
data.title: Audit docs and plans for stale About page references
